title: ERP Flow Knowledge Resource
description: One product flow ('how do I do X in AlphaSoft?') as a citable knowledge resource. Loaded by the Python runtime, matched deterministically against user questions, and injected into the turn prompt with its id as the citation (ai-planning/19 §7–8). Flows describe the PRODUCT only — never tenant data, never customer PII. If a flow and the product disagree, the flow is stale: fix it in the same cycle as the product change.

id.description: Globally unique dotted id, e.g. inventory.check-stock. This is the citation id — stable forever, never reused for different content.

version.description: Bumped on any content change; cited alongside the id so stale answers are traceable.

module.description: Primary taxonomy bucket. Cross-cutting flows pick the closest owner and list the rest in related_modules.

audience.description: Minimum role the flow is written for. The assistant may still cite a tenant_admin flow to a tenant_user to explain WHO can do it.

summary.description: What this flow accomplishes and the key concept behind it. Used for retrieval keywords and as prompt context.

permissions.description: module.resource.action triples copied from the owning module's config/permissions.php — never invented. Empty/omitted when the flow spans roles managed outside module permission bundles.

screen.description: Breadcrumb-style screen name as the user sees it, e.g. 'Inventory > Balances'. 'AI Copilot' is a valid screen for assistant-led steps.

route.description: Frontend route path, verified against the app router. Omit when there is no single route (e.g. AI Copilot steps).

related_ai_tools.description: Registered AI tool names (snake_case) that answer the live-data side of this flow. Only tools that exist in AiToolRegistry — proposed tools stay out until registered.

related_flows.description: Ids of other flow resources. Every reference must resolve — dangling ids are a validation error.

common_questions.description: Real phrasings users ask. Primary retrieval signal — write them the way people actually talk.

docs.description: Links to longer markdown documents (front-matter id 'docs.*') for prose that doesn't fit steps.

notes.description: Honest caveats and known limitations the assistant must convey rather than paper over (e.g. a tool that hides zero-stock rows). May reference planning docs for maintainers.
